interpretation_summary: The replication closely followed the preregistered plan: the SCORE_ALL_DATA dataset was filtered for preregistered exclusions, desirability was computed as the mean of three items, predictors were contrast-coded, and an OLS regression was run. Only minor code fixes (relative path and numeric coercion of the Exclusion flag) were required. The focal clarity × rating interaction was negative and statistically significant (b = -0.146, SE = 0.064, p = 0.022), echoing the original Study 2a estimate (b ≈ -0.17, SE = 0.08, p = 0.03). Effect magnitude is slightly smaller but direction and significance match, supporting the original claim on a larger independent sample (N = 376 vs. 149).

fidelity_assessment.method_alignment: High alignment: the executed analysis replicated the preregistered OLS model with the specified variables and exclusion rule; same contrast coding and outcome construction were applied.

deviations.issue_description: Hard-coded absolute file path in original script replaced with relative path; Exclusion column coerced to numeric.
deviations.impact: Low: purely technical fixes, no impact on analytic results.

deviations.issue_description: Replication used an independently collected SCORE dataset rather than the original MTurk sample.
deviations.impact: Moderate: larger sample and different recruitment source may slightly change effect size but tests the generalizability of the claim.

results_comparison.hypothesis_tested: The interaction between self-concept clarity (Clear = 1 vs. Conflicted = -1) and rating-distribution shape (Bimodal = 1 vs. Unimodal = -1) negatively predicts product desirability.
results_comparison.original_results: Interaction coefficient b = -0.17, SE = 0.08, t(123)=-2.15, p = 0.03 (Study 2a).
results_comparison.overall_answer: Yes – the replication reproduced a statistically significant negative interaction consistent in direction and similar in magnitude to the original finding.

replication_report: Replication successful: clarity × rating interaction remained negative and significant (b ≈ -0.15, p = 0.022) on a larger, independent sample, corroborating the original crossover desirability effect.

failure_handling.suggestions: Ensure future scripts reference data with relative paths and explicitly coerce mixed-type columns to numeric to avoid read/fit errors.
Document dataset provenance differences and, when possible, also replicate on the original MTurk data for direct comparability.

notes: Although the effect replicates, the overall variance explained is modest (R² = 0.039). Future work could test robustness across additional contexts and examine potential moderators influencing effect size.
